"scrollable-vertical": false, "children": [{"text": "If you are a new user or experiencing difficulties, we strongly recommend reading the FAQ first. You can view it by tapping on the corresponding button in the bottom right corner of the screen.

To switch between landscape and portrait modes, use the "Orientation" button under the Options menu. The screen orientation will be restored the next time you start SpeedView. If you would like to enable auto-rotation, you can find this option in the app's settings.

To switch to the Compass mode, tap on the linear compass. To enter the HUD mode, tap anywhere on the speedometer area. To open the Advanced screen, tap on the speed graph. You can find the corresponding buttons if you open the Options menu and select Dashboard. To return to the default screen while in the Compass or HUD mode, tap anywhere on that screen or use the Back button.

To reset the distance traveled, enable speed warning or track logging, or to change indicator views, tap on the corresponding field.

To quickly switch between speed limits, long-tap anywhere on one of the main dashboards (excluding Advanced). When done, long-tap anywhere on that screen (or use the Back button) to confirm your changes and return to the previous dashboard.

To quickly access the app's preferences, long-tap on the speed graph. To change display units and other preferences, tap on the Settings menu item.

If you want to use this app without a GPS connection, tap anywhere on the Startup screen to skip it. Note that you can pause the recording of the GPS data anytime by going to the Advanced dashboard, then tapping on the "Stop recording" button.

In case you are experiencing an erratic speed issue or the program freezes after acquiring a GPS fix, try setting the minimum accuracy value to 50 meters to ignore most inaccurate data.

If there are any other problems, please do not hesitate to submit a ticket or contact us directly at speedview@codesector.com.", "resource-id": "com.codesector.speedview.free:id/help_text", "ancestors": ["android.view.ViewGroup", "android.view.View", "java.lang.Object"], "clickable": false, "pressed": "not_pressed", "focusable": false, "long-clickable": false, "enabled": true, "bounds": [53, 221, 1387, 2168], "visibility": "visible", "content-desc": [null], "rel-bounds": [53, 53, 1387, 3719], "focused": false, "selected": false, "scrollable-vertical": false, "children": [{"text": "Can I use this app without a GPS connection?

Yes, you can review your saved data, use the compass and export your tracks as GPX files. Just tap on the startup screen to skip it.

How does the compass work?

When you are moving slower than 3 mph (about 5 km/h) the phone's orientation sensor is used to determine your heading. If you are moving faster than that the GPS is used to determine your heading.

What is HUD? How do I use it?

HUD stands for "head-up display" and was initially developed for military aviation. In this app, the HUD mode allows you to put your phone on your car's dashboard and see the speed reflected in the front glass. This can be useful when driving at night.

Why does it take so long to get a GPS fix?

The time required to get a GPS fix is dependent on the number of satellites in view and reception strength of each GPS satellite. This process normally takes 1-2 minutes, however you should not worry if it takes up to 20 minutes on the first occasion. If the receiver in your phone will still not acquire a fix, you might want to restart your phone. Also note that GPS will not work through heated or heat reflective windscreens. This is due to a metal layer in them which stops the signal.

The app lags, off by a few mph, etc.

GPS accuracy depends on a number of factors, most notably signal to noise ratio, satellite position, atmospheric conditions and obstructions of satellite signals. These factors can create errors in your perceived location. For example, objects such as buildings or mountains between the satellite and the receiver can produce an error up to 30 meters.

It drains the battery. Can you add a powersafe mode, only query GPS speed?

No, that is not how GPS works. In basic terms, GPS satellites send out an identifying signal, a GPS receiver picks up that signal and then the software can determine things such as longitude and latitude, direction, speed, etc.

How does the track logging feature work?

It generates a separate log file for each day that you use the app with the track logging option turned on. After a while, SpeedView erases the outdated log files, leaving only the most recent three. When exporting, you will be presented with a choice of which one to convert to the GPX format.", "resource-id": "com.codesector.speedview.free:id/faq_text", "adapter-view": false, "pointer":
